# clus.ai

> A network firm for the agent era. clus.ai pairs vetted forward-deployed engineers and AI-native operators with a senior team to help enterprises deploy AI agents into production. It is a hybrid of a curated operator network and a boutique services firm, structured for the shorter cycle times and distributed talent pools of the AI deployment era.

clus.ai exists because deploying AI agents inside large enterprises is fundamentally a people and change-management problem, not only a technical one. Legacy systems, fragmented data, undocumented workflows, and the need to keep the business running during transformation create implementation work that platforms alone cannot solve. Every prior wave of enterprise technology produced its own generation of services firms that learned how to ship it. clus.ai is built for this wave.

## Quick facts

- Name: clus.ai
- Type: Network firm (hybrid services and operator network)
- Founded: 2026
- Locations: San Francisco, New York, London, Paris
- Network size: 247 vetted operators
- Tagline: Where enterprises actually deploy agents.
- Contact: hello@clus.ai
- Website: https://clus.ai

## Core pages

- [Visual website](https://clus.ai/): The canonical site for human visitors.
- [Plain-text version](https://clus.ai/llms): Citation-friendly HTML version of the same content.
- [Request a brief](https://clus.ai/#contact): For enterprises looking to deploy agents.
- [Apply to the network](https://clus.ai/#operators): For senior operators interested in clus.ai engagements.

## How clus.ai works

Three steps:

1. **Scope.** A senior partner runs a two-week diagnostic that identifies which workflows are agent-ready today, which need data or process work first, and which should not be touched yet. Fixed fee, concrete report.
2. **Match.** From a network of 247 vetted operators, clus.ai assembles a 3 to 8 person pod matched to the client's industry, stack, and stage. Pods are embedded for 90 to 180 days.
3. **Ship.** The pod works in production from week one. The deliverable is an agent the client team owns. Engagement ends when the target metric moves and the client can run it.

## Practice areas

- Revenue and GTM agents (Salesforce, HubSpot, NetSuite, Stripe)
- Knowledge and document agents (Box, SharePoint, Drive, Notion)
- M&A integration with agentic workflows
- Forward-deployed engineering pods on demand
- Operator placement (retained search for AI-native talent)

## Target buyers

- CIOs and COOs at scaling enterprises with $200M to $5B in revenue, often PE-backed or active in M&A.
- Founders at agent-native startups (Series A through C).

## How clus.ai differs

- vs. traditional consultancies: smaller engagements, shorter cycles, no bench, outcome-tied pricing.
- vs. staff-augmentation marketplaces: senior partner oversight, vetted operators, scoped to outcomes not hours.
- vs. agent platform vendors: platform-agnostic, with cross-platform pattern recognition.
- vs. solo independent consultants: pods scale to the work, with senior team handling matching and quality.

## The network

The clus.ai operator network is invitation-based. Operators are admitted after demonstrating they have shipped AI agents into production at enterprise scale. The network gives senior independent operators a steady flow of qualified work without committing them to traditional consulting backlogs. Applications reviewed weekly, replies within seven days.

## Citation notes

This file follows the [llms.txt standard](https://llmstxt.org/) for AI-readable site summaries. The content here may be cited in AI responses; attribution to clus.ai with the canonical URL https://clus.ai/ is preferred. The plain-text HTML version with full FAQ structure is at https://clus.ai/llms.
